meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome the 'no project ideas' block. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' block is a failure of process, not creativity. By utilizing a robust SaaS starter kit, you shift focus from 'what to build' to 'who to serve.' Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate your MVP in hours, not weeks, focusing strictly on high-revenue micro-SaaS niches.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You have the technical hunger and the drive to build, but you are paralyzed by the endless noise of 'what's next.' Most founders drown in analysis paralysis, waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration that never comes, while the market opportunity for simple, effective software utilities remains wide open.

Relief arrives when you stop chasing 'the next unicorn' and start building 'the next utility.' By leveraging a proven SaaS starter kit, you eliminate the friction of authentication, database configuration, and payment integration. We aren't just building projects; we are deploying automated revenue engines. It is time to stop thinking and start shipping.

steps.title: Define Your Micro-SaaS Niche
steps.description: Identify a small, painful problem in a B2B vertical. Focus on workflows that involve manual spreadsheet work or repetitive data entry.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate 5 micro-SaaS ideas for small business owners that solve repetitive manual data tasks. Include target market, monetization strategy, and core MVP features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Research top 10 pain points for freelance accountants and suggest 3 SaaS solutions.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Use your starter kit to generate the auth and database layer. This ensures you start with a production-ready foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Next.js SaaS starter kit with Supabase authentication, Stripe integration, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure the directory structure is optimized for rapid feature deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure using the standard SaaS starter kit template.'

steps.title: Implement the Core Value Loop
steps.description: Focus strictly on the one action that provides value. If it is an AI tool, build the prompt-to-result path immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer for processing user input through an LLM. Implement error handling and rate limiting for API requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Create a service/openai.ts file to handle structured data extraction from user-uploaded PDFs.'

steps.title: Integrate Payments Early
steps.description: Don't wait until launch to build the billing. Connect Stripe now to ensure your business model is actually viable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler to manage subscription tiers and user access levels based on payment status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement Stripe checkout flow in the subscription page and add webhook listener for subscription.created events.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Push your MVP to Vercel or your provider of choice. The goal is to get a live URL in the hands of your first user within 48 hours.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist for a Next.js SaaS app including environment variables, database migrations, and domain configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify build health and generate a summary of remaining environment variables needed for production deployment.'

pro_tips: Never build custom auth; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 20+ hours of security engineering.
Ship the 'ugly' version first. If users won't pay for the raw utility, they won't pay for the design.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your payment logic, not your UI components.
Focus on 'Time-to-First-Value.' If a user can't experience the core benefit in under 60 seconds, your UI is bloated.
Every feature you add should be tied to a request from a paying user, not a hunch from your brain.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 80% of your time on cold outreach to your target niche. Collect feedback, iterate the prompt logic in your codebase using Claude Code, and scale your subscription tiers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation and the process to stop dreaming and start shipping. The market rewards speed and utility above all else. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
